# youtrack_notification_settings (Resource)

YouTrack mail server configuration

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "youtrack_notification_settings" "example" {
  is_enabled    = "true"
  mail_protocol = "SMTP"
  host          = "mailpit-smtp"
  port          = 1025
  anonymous     = true
  login         = "test@youtrack.com"
  from          = "no-reply@youtrack.com"
  reply_to      = "no-reply@youtrack.com"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `anonymous` (Boolean) Enable/disable anonymous access
- `from` (String) Email address to use in the From field
- `host` (String) Mail server DNS name or IP address
- `is_enabled` (Boolean) Enable/disable the notification
- `mail_protocol` (String) Mail protocol to use (e.g., SMTP, IMAP)
- `port` (Number) Mail server port

### Optional

- `login` (String) Login for mail server authentication
- `reply_to` (String) Email address to use in the Reply-To field

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Notification settings identifier
- `last_updated` (String) Timestamp of the last update

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Mail server is a singleton resource.
# You can use any value for the import ID (it will be ignored).
# The resource will fetch the current mail server configuration.
terraform import youtrack_notification_settings.server_config global
```
